title: Senecio salisburgensis
intro: Senecio salisburgensis, also known as Alpine Ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial protection. The bioactive constituents of Senecio salisburgensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alpine Ragwort is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional folk medicine, particularly for treating skin conditions and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Senecio salisburgensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the Senecio genus, which comprises a large number of species commonly known as ragworts or groundsels. The plant is native to the Eastern Alps, specifically in Austria and possibly other surrounding regions. Its classification is further divided into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Senecio salisburgensis is a relatively rare species, making its classification and distribution of interest to botanists and plant enthusiasts.
